sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Company (TSCO): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) based on its strong business model, consistent demand, and disciplined financial execution. TSCO has shown significant growth in revenues, operating profits, and net income, with a compelling dividend growth history. While currently trading above its historical valuation, its strong financials and operational discipline make it suitable for patient dividend investors.

feed.title: Edison International (EIX): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Edison International (EIX), highlighting its attractiveness following a significant share price decline, which is attributed to investor fears over potential Eaton Fire liabilities. The thesis emphasizes the company's strong regulated-utility model, predictable earnings, and substantial financial protections within California's regulatory framework, particularly the $21 billion Wildfire Fund. Despite concerns, EIX is presented as an undervalued opportunity poised for recovery, with an implied value of $79.11.

feed.title: Kroger expects to reach e-commerce profitability in 2026
feed.summary: Kroger anticipates its online business will achieve profitability in 2026, driven by an estimated $400 million in savings from closing automated fulfillment centers and increased focus on store-based fulfillment partnerships. This move comes as the grocer witnessed a 17% rise in digital sales during Q3, despite overall sales growing by less than 1%. The company is also actively searching for a new CEO and plans to accelerate capital investment in new stores.

feed.title: Cintas (Nasdaq: CTAS) sets Dec. 18 webcast for Q2 fiscal 2026 results
feed.summary: Cintas Corporation (Nasdaq: CTAS) announced it will release its fiscal year 2026 second quarter results on Thursday, December 18, 2025. The company will host a live webcast of a conference call to discuss these financial results, starting at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time on the same day. A replay of the webcast will be available on Cintas's website for two weeks following the live event.

feed.title: The AES Corporation (AES): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on The AES Corporation (AES), highlighting its compelling contrarian opportunity in the U.S. utilities sector despite a recent share price decline. The thesis points to AES's robust returns, deeply discounted valuation multiples, and strategic shift towards renewable energy as key drivers for potential upside, contrasting its metrics favorably against competitors like NextEra and Southern Company.

feed.title: Big Yellow Group Ends Acquisition Talks with Blackstone
feed.summary: Big Yellow Group (GB:BYG), a leading UK self-storage company, has terminated discussions with Blackstone Europe LLP regarding a potential cash acquisition offer. The decision was based on Big Yellow's strong performance and strategic direction, concluding there was no basis to continue talks. Big Yellow expresses confidence in its current business model, and a recent analyst rating suggests a "Hold" with a £1045.00 price target.

feed.title: IDEXX Laboratories Stock Rallies 75.6% YTD: What's Behind the Surge?
feed.summary: IDEXX Laboratories (IDXX) has seen its stock rally 75.6% year-to-date, significantly outperforming its industry and the S&P 500, driven by strong strategic execution, new business gains, and high customer retention. The surge is primarily attributed to robust momentum in its Companion Animal Group (CAG) Diagnostics business, which has shown consistent recurring revenue growth above sector levels and benefited from increased test volumes and an expanding installed base of premium instruments. The company's cloud-based products and pet owner engagement application, Vello, are also key growth drivers, despite concerns about global macroeconomic conditions and currency fluctuations.

feed.title: JAKKS Pacific Teams Up with The Hershey Company for Licensing Partnership
feed.summary: JAKKS Pacific is collaborating with The Hershey Company to launch a new line of collectible plush bag charms featuring iconic candy designs. The "Charming" line will include designs inspired by Twizzlers, Jolly Ranchers, Hershey’s Kisses, and Reese’s Peanut Butter Cups, with the first wave, Hershey's Kisses, launching exclusively at CVS on December 26th. This licensing deal aims to combine beloved treats with self-expression for consumers.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (ICE): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (ICE), highlighting its strong moat in financial infrastructure through its NYSE exchange, clearinghouses, and global data business. Despite a recent stock depreciation, the thesis asserts that ICE's "own the casino" model, with growth catalysts in digital ecosystems like the U.S. mortgage market and ESG markets, ensures its continued prosperity regardless of market conditions.

feed.title: Grab Unbeatable Cyber Monday Cruise Offers from Royal Caribbean and Discover Stunning Destinations at Rock-Bottom Prices
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean has launched Cyber Monday cruise deals starting at $126, including complimentary fares for third and fourth passengers on select trips. This promotion offers affordable access to destinations like the Bahamas, Caribbean, Alaska, and Europe, making luxurious cruise vacations accessible for solo travelers, couples, and families. The deals provide a perfect opportunity for budget-conscious travelers to experience all-inclusive adventures at sea.

feed.title: FCC approves AT&T $1 billion purchase of spectrum from US Cellular
feed.summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has approved AT&T's $1.02 billion acquisition of wireless spectrum licenses from U.S. Cellular. This approval came after AT&T committed to ending its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) programs, a condition the FCC has imposed since the Trump administration. The FCC stated that the deal will enhance AT&T's network coverage, capacity, and performance.

feed.title: WELL: UBS Raises Price Target to $232, Maintains Buy Rating | WE
feed.summary: UBS has maintained a 'Buy' rating for Welltower (WELL) and increased its price target to $232, up from $203, marking a 14.29% hike. This decision follows similar positive adjustments from other major analysts including Wells Fargo, Morgan Stanley, Scotiabank, Evercore ISI Group, and Cantor Fitzgerald. Welltower, a diversified healthcare REIT, owns a portfolio of 2,447 properties across senior housing, medical office, and skilled nursing sectors in multiple countries.

feed.title: Transcript : Stanley Black & Decker, Inc. Presents at Goldman Sachs Industrials and Materials Conference 2025, Dec-04-2025 11
feed.summary: Stanley Black & Decker, Inc. presented at the Goldman Sachs Industrials and Materials Conference 2025 on December 4, 2025, with CFO Pat Hallinan leading the presentation. The transcript is available to subscribers of S&P Capital IQ. The article also includes a list of recent news concerning Stanley Black & Decker, such as executive appointments and analyst rating changes.

feed.title: PTA-PVR: BAWAG Group AG: Release according to article 135 section 2 BörseG
feed.summary: BAWAG Group AG has released a notification regarding major holdings, as per article 135 section 2 BörseG. BlackRock, Inc. reported crossing a threshold on December 2, 2025, adjusting their total voting rights to 6.13% of BAWAG Group AG's 77,000,000 total voting rights. This announcement details the distribution of voting rights through shares and financial instruments, along with the full chain of controlled undertakings for BlackRock, Inc.

feed.title: Fastenal (FAST): Valuation Check After Strong November Net Sales and Daily Sales Growth
feed.summary: Fastenal (FAST) recently reported a 6% increase in November net sales and a 12% rise in daily sales, sparking investor interest in its valuation. The stock, currently around $41.22, has seen its price cool off while still showing strong long-term momentum. Despite a "modestly positive" narrative fair value of $43.46, its P/E ratio of 38.6 times suggests it may be pricey compared to the industry average, raising concerns about multiple compression if growth falters.

feed.title: Domino’s Pizza, Inc. (DPZ): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Domino's Pizza, Inc. (DPZ), highlighting its aggressive market positioning, strong franchisee economics, and robust supply chain as key drivers of growth. Despite a recent stock price depreciation, the company's financial efficiency and value-driven strategy make it a compelling long-term investment with potential to reach $500 per share by the end of 2026. The thesis also noted DPZ's expansion into delivery aggregators and its consistently high returns on assets and invested capital.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Earnings Are Imminent; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ: ULTA) is set to release its third-quarter earnings after the market closes on Thursday, December 4th, with analysts anticipating Q3 earnings of $4.60 per share and revenue of $2.7 billion. Several highly-rated analysts have recently updated their price targets and ratings for Ulta Beauty, including Christopher Horvers of JP Morgan, Dana Telsey of Telsey Advisory Group, Ike Boruchow of Wells Fargo, Susan Anderson of Canaccord Genuity, and Adrienne Yih of Barclays. The company's shares closed at $544.52 on Wednesday, and Christopher DelOrefice was recently named CFO.

feed.title: Kroger's next CEO will come from outside the company
feed.summary: Kroger announced that its next CEO, to be named in the first quarter of 2026, will be an external hire with experience in retail transformation and large-scale operations. This decision follows the abrupt resignation of former CEO Rodney McMullen in March 2025 due to a company ethics investigation. Interim CEO Ron Sargent confirmed the search process is thorough and has identified several highly qualified candidates.

feed.title: Duke Energy (NYSE: DUK) joins DOE $400M SMR effort, plans Belews Creek permit
feed.summary: Duke Energy announced its continued participation in a U.S. Department of Energy cost-share initiative aimed at deploying GE Vernova Hitachi's BWRX-300 small modular reactor (SMR) technology, following a $400 million grant to Tennessee Valley Authority. This collaboration supports Duke's new nuclear strategy, emphasizing grid reliability and carbon-free generation. Duke plans to submit an early site permit application for potential SMR deployment at its Belews Creek site in North Carolina by the end of 2025.

feed.title: Tractor Supply is coming to Lawrence!
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company is set to open its first Mercer County location in the Lawrence Shopping Center in Lawrence Township, New Jersey. The new store will occupy the southern part of the current Burlington store, offering a wide range of products including agricultural items, home goods, and pet supplies. While a specific opening date is not yet announced, the company hopes to be operational in 2026, contributing to the shopping center's ongoing revitalization.

feed.title: PCA to shut down machines in Washington, affecting 200 jobs
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (PCA) announced it will permanently shut down the No. 2 paper machine and kraft pulping facilities at its Wallula, Washington, containerboard mill, resulting in 200 layoffs. The decision is driven by high operating costs, with PCA aiming to streamline operations and improve competitiveness. The company plans to offset the reduced capacity through enhancements at other mills and expects to incur $205 million in restructuring charges.

feed.title: MGM Resorts (NYSE: MGM) opens Gymkhana, first Indian fine dining on Las Vegas Strip
feed.summary: ARIA Resort & Casino, a property of MGM Resorts, has officially opened Gymkhana, marking the first Indian fine-dining restaurant on the Las Vegas Strip and the London-based Two Michelin-starred restaurant's U.S. debut. This venture, a partnership with JKS Restaurants, aims to enhance ARIA's reputation as a premier dining destination by offering a world-class culinary experience with a focus on North Indian cuisine, a luxurious atmosphere, and an extensive beverage program. The new establishment is part of MGM's broader strategy to differentiate its properties through high-end experiences, building on recent property upgrades and branding initiatives.

feed.title: Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) Declares $0.62 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.62 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record on February 13, 2026. This marks the 15th consecutive year the company has increased its dividend, offering an annualized yield of 2.9%. While the current payout ratio is high at 82.9%, analysts anticipate an improved payout ratio of 67.8% next year based on projected EPS of $3.66.

feed.title: GE HealthCare, Mayo Clinic launch digital twin project for personalizing radiation cancer therapy
feed.summary: GE HealthCare and the Mayo Clinic are collaborating on GEMINI-RT, a project to develop personalized radiation therapy for cancer patients using AI digital twins. This initiative will integrate imaging, AI, and patient monitoring to automate radiation dose planning, predict treatment outcomes, and incorporate remote monitoring for side effects. The goal is to create tailored treatments by modeling individual patient journeys.

feed.title: DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) Increases Dividend to $1.17 Per Share
feed.summary: DTE Energy Company (NYSE:DTE) has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $1.165 per share, representing a 6.9% increase from its previous dividend. This translates to an annualized dividend of approximately $4.36 and a yield of 3.5%, with the company planning to pay shareholders of record on December 15th, 2025. The dividend is supported by a sustainable payout ratio of around 56% and positive EPS guidance for FY2025 and FY2026.

feed.title: Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) Sets New 1-Year High - Here's Why
feed.summary: Shares of Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) recently hit a new 52-week high of $192.88, driven by strong quarterly earnings that beat analyst estimates and positive analyst upgrades. The company reported EPS of $0.85 against an expected $0.78 and revenue of $769.2 million against $744.2 million. Analysts currently have a "Moderate Buy" rating for Teradyne with an average price target of $176.88, with some targets reaching $225.

feed.title: Raymond James Announces $2B Share Repurchase Plan & 8% Dividend Hike
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial, Inc. (RJF) has announced a new $2 billion share repurchase program, replacing a previous $1.5 billion plan, and an 8% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to 54 cents per share. This move underscores RJF's commitment to returning value to shareholders, supported by its strong cash position and consistent earnings. The company has a history of regular dividend increases over the past decade.

feed.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang admits he works 7 days a week, including holidays, in a constant 'state of anxiety' out of fear of going bankrupt
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang revealed he works seven days a week, including holidays, driven by a persistent "state of anxiety" and fear of the company going bankrupt, despite Nvidia's current success as the world's most valuable company. He attributes his motivation more to fear of failure than desire for success, a mindset he even shared with Stanford students, emphasizing the importance of adversity for resilience. Huang recounts a near collapse in the mid-1990s as a pivotal moment that cemented his constant vigilance.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley’s Top European Pharmaceutical Picks for Investors By Investing.com
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has identified key European pharmaceutical companies with varied investment opportunities. AstraZeneca and Sanofi are highlighted for strong earnings growth and attractive valuations despite some challenges, while Bayer received an upgrade due to potential litigation de-risking and improving fundamentals. GSK remains Underweight due to longer-term headwinds, and Roche is viewed with caution despite recent successes. Novartis is seen to have accelerating growth potential beyond its short-term outlook.

feed.title: Merck (NYSE: MRK) gets FDA conditional OK for EXZOLT CATTLE-CA1 vs New World screwworm
feed.summary: Merck Animal Health has received conditional FDA approval for EXZOLT™ CATTLE-CA1, an innovative pour-on solution for cattle. This marks the first isoxazoline parasiticide conditionally approved for the prevention and treatment of New World screwworm larvae and control of cattle fever ticks. The product addresses a significant threat to U.S. agriculture and will be available by prescription in early 2026.

feed.title: Attention Long-Term Shareholders of Cytokinetics, Inc.
feed.summary: Grabar Law Office is investigating claims on behalf of long-term shareholders of Cytokinetics, Inc. (NASDAQ: CYTK), Molina Healthcare, Inc. (NYSE: MOH), Synopsys, Inc. (NASDAQ: SNPS), and WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. (NASDAQ: WBTN) due to alleged breaches of fiduciary duties by certain officers and directors. The investigations focus on various issues including misleading statements regarding FDA applications, undisclosed material facts, inflated growth figures, and misleading IPO disclosures. Shareholders who purchased shares before specific dates are encouraged to contact the firm for potential corporate reforms and compensation.

feed.title: Wall Street Bulls Look Optimistic About Lam Research (LRCX): Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts are optimistic about Lam Research (LRCX), with an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.69, suggesting a strong buy. However, the article advises against relying solely on ABR due to potential positive bias from brokerage firms and instead recommends using the Zacks Rank, which indicates a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) for LRCX due to recent positive earnings estimate revisions.

feed.title: Hormel sees pop in sales to finish 2025, profitability loss
feed.summary: Hormel Foods Corp. reported higher sales but a decline in profitability for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025. Despite top-line growth driven by brand relevance and a strong value-added portfolio, the company faced challenges from input cost inflation. Hormel is taking steps to improve profitability, including pricing initiatives and administrative expense reductions, and projects net sales for 2026 to be between $12.2 billion to $12.5 billion.

feed.title: Molecular Cytogenetics Market Intelligence Report (2020-2030) Featuring Key Players Danaher, MetaSystems, Agilent Technologies, Abbott Labs, Bio-Rad Labs and More - ResearchAndMarkets.com
feed.summary: The global Molecular Cytogenetics Market, valued at USD 2.24 Billion in 2024, is projected to grow to USD 3.62 Billion by 2030, exhibiting a CAGR of 8.33%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing prevalence of genetic conditions and oncological diseases, alongside the rising demand for personalized medicine. Key challenges include the high cost of instrumentation and the need for specialized expertise, while key trends involve the integration of AI and automation to enhance efficiency and precision in workflows.

feed.title: Today & Tomorrow - Commercial, powering the integration engine %
feed.summary: This article discusses the role of Phillips 66's Commercial sector in powering the company's integration engine. It highlights how strong assets, global scale, and talent contribute to Commercial's impact, emphasizing its competitive differentiators, integrated downstream portfolio, and the capabilities of its people. The discussion features Brian Mandell, EVP of Marketing and Commercial, and Mark Hughes, SVP of Commercial, who explain how their approach creates a unique and diversified value proposition for Phillips 66 in global markets.

feed.title: Cooper Companies Likely To Report Higher Q4 Earnings; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (NASDAQ: COO) is expected to report higher Q4 earnings of $1.11 per share and $1.06 billion in revenue after the market closes on Thursday, December 4th. This is an increase from the previous year's $1.04 per share and $1.02 billion revenue. The company's board also approved a $1 billion increase in its share repurchase program on September 17th.

feed.title: Atmos Energy director Donohue sells $77k in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Atmos Energy Director Sean Donohue sold 450 shares for approximately $77,017 on December 3, 2025, reducing his direct ownership to 876.598 shares. This insider transaction follows the company's strong fourth-quarter financial results, including an EPS of $1.07 and revenue of $754.64 million, which led Mizuho to raise its price target to $180.

feed.title: Walmart Inc. (WMT): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article presents a bull case for Walmart Inc. (WMT), highlighting its transformation into a digitally enabled, margin-expanding platform. The analysis emphasizes Walmart's omnichannel strategy, including leveraging its stores as fulfillment centers and developing higher-margin profit streams like advertising and Walmart+ memberships. The article suggests that Walmart's current valuation premium is justified by its strategic initiatives, despite the stock's sensitivity to execution risks.

feed.title: Big Tech Stocks: Earnings Season Winners and Losers
feed.summary: This article reviews the performance of several major tech stocks following their latest earnings reports, highlighting both winners and losers in a mixed market response to the AI boom. Alphabet emerged as a winner with an 8% surge due to strong AI execution, while Meta Platforms and Palantir saw declines despite strong results, primarily due to concerns over AI spending and high valuations, respectively. Nvidia also slipped after reporting, despite impressive earnings, which analysts attribute to broader market skepticism about AI valuations.

feed.title: NXP Semiconductors N.V. (VNX.DE): Riding a Significant Uptrend on Germany’s XETRA
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors N.V. (VNX.DE) is showing strong momentum on Germany's XETRA, with an 8.68% increase to €194.00, driven by robust trading volumes and favorable technical indicators. The company boasts solid financials, including a market cap of €49.49 billion and an ROE of 21.63%, despite challenges in the broader semiconductor sector. NXP's diverse product range and strategic position in automotive and IoT solutions contribute to its resilience and optimistic market outlook.

feed.title: Truist Securities Raises Price Target for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) | HST Stock News
feed.summary: Truist Securities upgraded its price target for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) to $21.00 from $19.00, while maintaining a 'Hold' rating. This adjustment reflects analyst Patrick Scholes' continued outlook on the stock. Host Hotels & Resorts is an owner of numerous upper-upscale and luxury hotel properties, primarily in the United States.

feed.title: Danaher (DHR) Valuation Check After Analyst Upgrade and 2026 Earnings Recovery Narrative
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's upgrade has put Danaher (DHR) on watchlists, fueled by optimism for a 2026 recovery through core revenue growth and earnings leverage. While currently appearing undervalued at $228.46 against a narrative fair value of $254.20, its P/E ratio of 46.1x suggests a tougher valuation story compared to its industry. The long-term narrative focuses on precision medicine and AI-assisted diagnostics driving EBITDA expansion, though risks like China policy shifts and biotech funding weakness could impact recovery.

feed.title: Keysight unveils new FieldFox handheld analyzer with 120 MHz streaming
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) has introduced its N99xxD-Series FieldFox Handheld Analyzer with 120 MHz gap-free IQ streaming, designed for complex radio frequency environments. The analyzer offers laboratory-grade precision with field-ready durability and AI capabilities for automatic classification of wireless standards. This launch follows strong fiscal fourth-quarter results for Keysight, with analysts raising price targets due to robust demand and growth in AI and data center markets.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Has Lowered Expectations for Chemours (NYSE:CC) Stock Price
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has reduced its price target for Chemours (NYSE:CC) from $15.00 to $13.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, which implies a minimal upside. This move reflects lowered expectations, consistent with other firms that have recently cut their price objectives for the specialty chemicals company. Chemours recently missed quarterly EPS estimates and reported a negative net margin, with its shares trading around $12.95.

feed.title: Medtronic’s Hugo surgical robot earns FDA clearance
feed.summary: Medtronic's Hugo surgical robot has received FDA clearance for urologic procedures in the U.S., positioning it as a significant challenger to Intuitive Surgical in the robotic surgery market. This authorization allows hospitals more choice in expanding their robotic programs, with plans to extend the robot's use to general surgery and gynecology in the future. The company anticipates this expansion will help revive growth in its medical/surgical portfolio and leverage its modular design and digital technologies as key differentiators.

feed.title: NextEra Energy, Inc. (NEE): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on NextEra Energy, Inc. (NEE), highlighting its strong financial and operational performance, consistent EPS growth outlook, and diversified portfolio including renewables and storage. The thesis emphasizes the company's ability to cater to increasing data-center and AI energy demand, with significant rerating potential despite identified risks.

feed.title: Why Palantir And Nvidia's Infrastructure Move Matters More Than Any Model
feed.summary: Palantir Technologies, in partnership with Nvidia and CenterPoint Energy, is shifting the focus of AI growth from model development to infrastructure through its "Chain Reaction" initiative. They argue that the bottleneck to AI innovation is now power and compute, not algorithms. This collaboration highlights a strategic pivot towards ensuring scalable energy and compute environments are available to sustain the rapidly expanding AI landscape, impacting future investment and defining the next phase of AI companies.

feed.title: Boston Scientific gets European nod for Farapoint catheter
feed.summary: Boston Scientific recently received CE mark for its Farapoint pulsed field ablation (PFA) catheter, allowing it to be marketed in Europe for treating right atrial flutter (AFL). This expands the indications for its Farapulse PFA platform, which was initially approved for AFib. The company aims to become a top player in the electrophysiology space, leveraging the Farapoint catheter's demonstrated efficacy and safety in preventing AFL recurrence.

feed.title: A $5B Tailwind: How Ukraine's Allies Are Poised to Boost Defense ETFs
feed.summary: The ongoing conflict in Ukraine is creating significant tailwinds for U.S. defense contractors and defense-focused ETFs. With direct U.S. aid paused, allied nations are committing billions towards purchasing U.S. weapons for Ukraine, ensuring a steady revenue stream for manufacturers. This shift emphasizes the strategic advantage of diversified defense ETFs for investors looking to capitalize on sustained global defense spending.

feed.title: EBay's New Sports Card Price Guide Could Change The Game for Collectors
feed.summary: EBay Inc. is launching a new pricing tool for trading cards, utilizing actual marketplace data to provide collectors with accurate valuations. This feature, accessible via the "My eBay" mobile app, allows users to scan cards and view two years of transaction history for both raw and graded items. The initiative aims to address historical challenges in pricing accuracy within the trading card market, a sector that significantly contributed to eBay's strong Q3 growth.
